name: Rock and Roll
description: Rock 'n' roll icons and bands from many eras

questions.q: Steven Tyler is the flamboyant lead singer of which Boston rock band?

source.notes: Fronts Aerosmith since 1970.

questions.q: Dave Grohl, former drummer of Nirvana, is the frontman of which rock band?

source.notes: Founded Foo Fighters in 1994 after Kurt Cobain's death.

questions.q: James Hetfield is the rhythm guitarist and lead vocalist of which thrash-metal band?

source.notes: Co-founded Metallica with drummer Lars Ulrich in 1981.

questions.q: Bono is the lead singer of which Irish rock band?

source.notes: Formed in Dublin in 1976.

questions.q: Which troubled singer-songwriter fronted the grunge band Nirvana?

source.notes: Died by suicide in April 1994 at age 27.

questions.q: Eddie Vedder is the lead vocalist of which Seattle grunge band?

source.notes: Joined Pearl Jam in 1990; debut album 'Ten' released 1991.

questions.q: Axl Rose is the frontman of which late-'80s Los Angeles hard-rock band?

source.notes: Debut album 'Appetite for Destruction' (1987) is one of the best-selling debut albums of all time.

questions.q: Robert Plant was the distinctive lead vocalist of which legendary British rock band?

source.notes: Fronted Led Zeppelin 1968–1980.

questions.q: Charlie Watts played drums for which legendary rock band from 1963 until his death in 2021?

source.notes: Widely regarded as one of rock's greatest drummers.

questions.q: Roger Daltrey provided lead vocals for which legendary British rock band?

source.notes: The Who formed in London in 1964.

questions.q: Stevie Nicks became famous as a singer-songwriter in which hugely successful soft-rock band?

source.notes: Joined Fleetwood Mac with Lindsey Buckingham in 1974.

questions.q: Trent Reznor is the sole official member of which influential industrial rock band?

source.notes: Formed 1988; Reznor also scored 'The Social Network' and 'Soul'.

questions.q: Julian Casablancas is the lead singer of which New York-based indie-rock band?

source.notes: Debut album 'Is This It' (2001) helped launch the 2000s garage-rock revival.

questions.q: Before Brian Johnson took over, who was AC/DC's original lead singer, who died in 1980?

source.notes: Scott died in London in February 1980; 'Back in Black' was recorded with Brian Johnson shortly after.

questions.q: Bruce Dickinson is the longtime operatic lead singer of which British heavy-metal band?

source.notes: Joined Iron Maiden in 1981; also a commercial airline pilot.

questions.q: The haunting post-punk anthem 'Love Will Tear Us Apart' was released in 1980 by which band?

source.notes: Released shortly after singer Ian Curtis's death.

questions.q: The Britpop rivals Oasis hail from which English city?

source.notes: Formed in Manchester in 1991 by brothers Noel and Liam Gallagher.

questions.q: The Who's 1969 rock opera double album is titled what?

source.notes: Tells the story of 'that deaf, dumb, and blind kid'.

questions.q: The evocative 1967 hit 'A Whiter Shade of Pale' was released by which British band?

source.notes: Keith Reid's lyrics and Matthew Fisher's Bach-inspired organ motif.

questions.q: Billy Corgan fronted which 1990s alternative rock band named after a seasonal vegetable?

source.notes: Formed in Chicago 1988; hits include 'Tonight, Tonight' and '1979'.

questions.q: The 1998 ballad 'I Don't Want to Miss a Thing', from the movie 'Armageddon', was a #1 hit for which band?

source.notes: Written by Diane Warren; Aerosmith's first and only US #1.

questions.q: Paul Rodgers was the soulful frontman of which mid-1970s English blues-rock band, famous for 'Feel Like Makin' Love'?

source.notes: Formed 1973; Rodgers had earlier fronted Free.
